title: Using Summary columns for calculation in dashboards
excerpt: Hi All. Anyone now how to use a Summary Column as a measurement for Time Tracking. I would like to have TimeTracking at the Sub-Item level, summarized at the Item level, and that total used to measure Time. Thanks, Linco…

posts.author: Lincoln (Lincoln Graham-Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi All. Anyone now how to use a Summary Column as a measurement for Time Tracking. I would like to have TimeTracking at the Sub-Item level, summarized at the Item level, and that total used to measure Time. Thanks, Lincoln

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Lincoln ,

Welcome to the monday community!

Once you add the time tracking column to your subitems, click the three dots on the top right of the column and choose show summary on parent item.

image395×582 30.4 KB

Then a new column will appear on the main item showing the added tracked time of its subitems.

**For Dashboards, it is not currently possible to use the time tracking column. You would need to follow the same process as mentioned but with a numbers column, where it would count hours.

Hope this is what you are looking for!

Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com
